message.content: You are the synthesizer. Fanout results: [{"id":0,"primitive":"parallel-barrier","note":"waits for all parallel branches to finish"},{"id":1,"primitive":"concurrency-cap","note":"Limits maximum parallel concurrent operations"},{"id":2,"primitive":"schema-output","note":"Formats result into required data structure"}]. Pipeline results: ["Received stage-1 id=0, primitive=\"pipeline:stage-semantics\" for originalItem=\"stage-semantics\".","Received stage-1 id=1 for originalItem=\"no-barrier\" with primitive=\"pipeline:no-barrier\"."]. In at most 3 sentences, summarize which primitives were exercised and whether parallel preserved input order.

content.text: Two fanout primitives were exercised (parallel-barrier and concurrency-cap), plus schema-output for result formatting, while the pipeline exercised pipeline:stage-semantics and pipeline:no-barrier across two stages. The parallel fanout preserved input order, as evidenced by the items retaining their original id sequence (0, 1, 2). The pipeline also maintained order, with stage-1 results indexed consistently with their original items.
